title: Takeda — Contacts
html:
Takeda Contact Map

Governing Thought

Takeda US has approximately 5,000 employees, with insights and analytics teams distributed across multiple business units — GI, Rare Disease, Neuroscience, Oncology, and Plasma-Derived Therapies. Despite 11 active projects and a ~6-7 year partnership, the Takeda contact map at ZoomRx is largely unpopulated. No client contact names, titles, or working-style notes have been extracted from available SharePoint data. SOWs, proposals, and call notes from active projects are the primary enrichment sources. Building this contact map is the single highest-priority operational gap on the account — without it, the account team cannot (a) navigate Takeda's org structure for BD expansion, (b) identify cross-TA advocates who can introduce ZoomRx to new teams, or (c) protect the account during Takeda personnel transitions.

Organization Structure (Partial)

Based on Takeda's public organizational structure and the TAs covered by ZoomRx projects:

Takeda US (Cambridge, MA)
  |
  |--- GI Business Unit
  |      |--- Entyvio Franchise (IBD)
  |      |      |--- Commercial Insights / MR
  |      |      |--- Medical Affairs
  |      |--- Eohilia / Rare GI
  |      |      |--- [Contacts managing Rare GI PET]
  |      |--- Gattex (SBS)
  |             |--- [Contacts from historical PET/ATU/PCA work]
  |
  |--- Rare Disease BU
  |      |--- Takhzyro / HAE Franchise
  |      |      |--- [Contacts from HAE Medical Data Assessment]
  |      |--- Adzynma (TTP)
  |      |--- Livtencity (CMV)
  |
  |--- Neuroscience BU
  |      |--- Narcolepsy Pipeline
  |      |      |--- [Contacts from Conference Studies]
  |      |--- Epilepsy / Soticlestat
  |      |--- Vyvanse (ADHD — mature/declining)
  |
  |--- Oncology BU
  |      |--- [Historical: Ninlaro, Pevonedistat, Millennium/Takeda Onc]
  |      |--- [Contacts — likely lapsed given no recent oncology projects]
  |
  |--- Immunology / Inflammation
  |      |--- Zasocitinib (TAK-279) Pipeline
  |      |      |--- [Contacts from Color Pref RT, Global Pulse, Radiographic RT]
  |      |--- Plaque Psoriasis Team
  |             |--- [Contacts from PsO Medical Data Feedback Study]
  |
  |--- Hematology
  |      |--- Mezagitamab (IgAN)
  |             |--- [Contacts from Color Preference RT]
  |
  |--- Above-Brand / Innovation
  |      |--- [Contacts from AI Workshop — transcripts on file]
  |
  |--- Procurement / Vendor Management
         |--- [MSA / vendor agreement contacts — unknown]

Note: This org chart is inferred from project names and Takeda's public structure. No named contacts are available from the SharePoint scan. Populate from SOWs, call notes, and proposals.

Tier 1: Day-to-Day Contacts (Enrichment Needed)

These contacts should exist in SOWs and call notes for active projects. The account team should extract and populate.

[Populate] [Populate] [Populate] Zasocitinib Global Pulse SOW (Nov 2025) [Populate]

[Populate] [Populate] [Populate] PsO Medical Data Feedback Study (Oct 2025) [Populate]

Tier 2: Decision Makers (Public Leadership)

These are publicly available Takeda US leaders whose organizations likely govern MR vendor decisions.

Name Title What They Likely Control Relationship Status
Costa Saroukos CEO (designated, effective 2025) Corporate direction; not a direct contact No relationship
Julie Kim President, US Business Unit US commercial strategy No relationship
Ramona Sequeira President, Global Portfolio Division Global brand strategy and P&L No relationship
[US Head of Insights/MR] [Title TBD] All US MR vendor relationships — Takeda's Rhonda Harp equivalent Priority 1 contact to identify. This person is the gatekeeper for expanding ZoomRx across BUs.
[Head of GI Commercial] [Title TBD] Entyvio + Eohilia MR budgets Priority 2 — controls the largest franchise budget
[Head of Immunology/Inflammation] [Title TBD] Zasocitinib MR budgets Priority 3 — controls the highest-growth pipeline budget

Action: Identify and map Takeda US's Head of Market Research / Commercial Insights. This is the #1 relationship-building priority on the account — the equivalent of Rhonda Harp at Merck. Without this contact, BD expansion depends entirely on brand-level contacts who may not have cross-BU visibility.

Tier 3: ZoomRx Internal Team (Takeda-Facing)

Name Role Cluster Takeda Brands
AO [Populate] — Account owner
AD [Populate] — Day-to-day account management
Execution team [Populate] [Populate cluster] Active project delivery

Contact Map Enrichment Priority

Immediate (Next 30 Days)

Action Source Owner Impact
Extract client contacts from all active SOWs SharePoint — SOW/proposal files for 11 active projects AD Populates ~15-20 Tier 1 contacts across 5+ TAs
Extract contacts from Entyvio VERDICT call notes SharePoint — Project #1 (Apr 2026) call notes AD Entyvio franchise contacts — highest BD value
Extract contacts from zasocitinib SOWs + call notes SharePoint — Color Pref RT (Feb 2026), Global Pulse (Nov 2025) AD Immunology/Derm contacts — highest growth value
Extract contacts from AI Workshop transcripts SharePoint — AI Workshop (Oct 2025) transcripts AD Above-brand innovation contacts — strategic value
Identify Takeda US Head of Insights/MR LinkedIn research + cross-reference with SOW signatories AO #1 relationship gap on the account

Near-Term (Next 60 Days)

Action Source Owner Impact
Extract contacts from HAE Medical Data Assessment SharePoint — HAE MDA (Oct 2025) AD Rare Disease contacts
Map narcolepsy conference study contacts SharePoint — Narcolepsy conference SOWs AD Neuroscience contacts
Extract historical GI contacts from Gattex PET SOWs SharePoint — Gattex PET (2020-2025) AD Longest-tenured relationships; may have moved to senior roles
Check for Takeda procurement / MSA contact Internal ZoomRx records AO Affects pricing leverage and AI-enabled work
Cross-reference mezagitamab contacts with hematology org SharePoint — Mezagitamab Color Pref RT (Apr 2026) AD Hematology contacts — future launch opportunity

Medium-Term (Quarterly)

Action Source Owner Impact
Build Takeda US org chart (partial) SOW contacts + LinkedIn + Takeda IR AO Enables cross-BU BD strategy
Identify cross-TA advocates Recurring contacts across multiple projects AD Internal champions who can refer ZoomRx to new teams
Map medical affairs contacts separately from commercial SOW review (HAE MDA, AI Workshop) AD Opens MSL/MEE and medical data budget lines

Relationship Intelligence: Working Style (To Be Populated)

Once contacts are extracted, document the following for each:

  1. Communication preference — Email, Teams, phone calls? How formal?
  2. Decision style — Data-driven, relationship-driven, consensus-builder?
  3. Research sophistication — Understands MR methodology, or needs education?
  4. Innovation appetite — Open to new formats (AI, Color Pref RT), or conservative?
  5. Budget authority — Signs SOWs directly, or needs approval from above?
  6. Cross-TA visibility — Sees work across Takeda BUs, or siloed within one franchise?

Known Behavioral Signals (from Project Types)

Even without named contacts, the project types commissioned tell us about Takeda's buyer personality:

  • Color Preference RT (2 projects): The buyer values visual branding research — likely a marketing-oriented buyer, not pure MR
  • Radiographic RT: The buyer values clinical visual interpretation studies — likely medical affairs or clinical marketing
  • AI Workshop: Someone at Takeda authorized an innovation-forward engagement with ZoomRx — this person is a potential above-brand champion
  • Conference Studies (4 projects): The buyer values rapid-turnaround, conference-aligned research — likely a competitive intelligence or medical affairs role
  • Global Pulse: The buyer thinks globally (not just US) — likely a global marketing or global insights role

Contact Gaps: Risk Assessment

Gap Risk Level Why
No named contacts documented HIGH Cannot protect relationships during personnel transitions; cannot navigate for BD expansion
No Takeda US Head of Insights identified HIGH Cannot build above-brand relationship; BD expansion requires brand-by-brand selling without a champion
No procurement / MSA contact MEDIUM Cannot assess vendor status, pricing leverage, or AI addendum requirements
Historical contacts (Gattex, Ninlaro, Motegrity) not mapped MEDIUM Potential senior contacts who have moved to leadership roles; relationship value may have appreciated
No medical affairs contacts mapped MEDIUM HAE MDA and AI Workshop suggest medical affairs engagement, but contacts are invisible

Back to Accounts Hub | Home
